text:
No. There was no court finding in the Fox–Dominion case that validated the allegation that Dominion manipulated vote totals.

What the case established, procedurally, was essentially the opposite:

- The court made key pretrial rulings that the challenged statements about Dominion were false for purposes of the defamation case.
- The case did not go to a final trial verdict because Fox settled with Dominion.
- Because it settled, there was no trial judgment finding that Dominion had in fact manipulated votes.
- More broadly, the litigation did not produce any judicial validation of the vote-rigging allegations.

So the accurate summary is: no such validation was established in that case.
